title: Dissipative Spectral Form Factor of the Complex Elliptic Ginibre Ensemble across Various Non-Hermiticity Regimes
authors: Gernot Akemann, Sung-Soo Byun, Seungjoon Oh
abstract: We study the dissipative spectral form factor (DSFF) at complex time $T e^{iθ}$ for the complex elliptic Ginibre ensemble with non-Hermiticity parameter $τ\in [0,1)$. As the matrix dimension $N \to \infty$, we consider the natural scalings in both the time variable and the non-Hermiticity parameter, namely $T = O(N^γ)$ and $1 - τ= O(N^{-α})$. For all regimes $γ\ge 0$ and $α\ge 0$, we derive the precise asymptotic behaviour of both the disconnected and connected components of the DSFF. In particular, we explicitly characterise the dip--ramp--plateau structure, including the dip time and the Heisenberg time. In addition, we identify the mesoscopic regime $α\in (0,1)$, which interpolates between the behaviour of the DSFF of non-Hermitian random matrices and the spectral form factor (SFF) of Hermitian ensembles. We further provide an explicit description of the phase diagram, in which the ramp exhibits quadratic, linear, or intermediate behaviour depending on the scaling parameters.
summary: This paper provides a complete asymptotic characterization of the Dissipative Spectral Form Factor for the complex elliptic Ginibre ensemble. It maps out a phase diagram showing how the spectral statistics transition between Hermitian and non-Hermitian behaviors depending on the scaling of time and non-Hermiticity. The results rigorously define the scaling of key features like the Thouless and Heisenberg times.
main_problem: The paper aims to derive the mathematically rigorous large-N asymptotic behavior of the Dissipative Spectral Form Factor (DSFF) for the complex elliptic Ginibre ensemble across various non-Hermiticity regimes.
main_result: The authors identify a complete phase diagram for the DSFF, characterizing the dip-ramp-plateau structure and the transition between GUE-type (linear ramp) and GinUE-type (quadratic ramp) statistics, including the scaling of Thouless and Heisenberg times.
method: The study employs the framework of determinantal point processes, using orthogonal polynomial techniques, Christoffel-Darboux formulas, and uniform asymptotic expansions (Bessel, Airy, and exponential regimes) to analyze the large-N limit.
model_or_system: The model is the Complex Elliptic Ginibre Ensemble (eGinUE), a non-Hermitian random matrix model that interpolates between the Hermitian GUE limit and the strongly non-Hermitian Ginibre limit, representing open, dissipative quantum chaotic systems.
key_observables: Dissipative Spectral Form Factor (DSFF), split into connected and disconnected components; Thouless time (T_Th); Heisenberg time (T_H).
important_parameters: Non-Hermiticity parameter (tau); scaling parameters (alpha and gamma) for the non-Hermiticity and time variables; matrix dimension (N).
main_assumptions: The analysis is restricted to the large-N (asymptotic) limit and focuses on the simplest ensemble without additional symmetries.
figures_summary: Figure 1(a) shows the dip-ramp-plateau structure of the DSFF with characteristic time scales, and Figure 1(b) illustrates the universality crossover in the (alpha, gamma) phase plane; Figure 2 provides numerical illustration in the strong non-Hermiticity regime.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the definition of the eGinUE and the DSFF, followed by the decomposition into connected and disconnected components. It then proceeds through a detailed asymptotic analysis across four regimes (Bessel, Oscillatory, Airy, and Exponential) to identify the phase diagram and characteristic time scales, concluding with mathematical appendices on expansion coefficients.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant for researchers of open quantum systems as it provides a rigorous description of how dissipation and non-Hermiticity affect spectral correlations and the emergence of universal features like the ramp and plateau in quantum chaotic dynamics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. Phase diagrams of the DSFF in the (α, γ)–plane, where the non-Hermiticity and time scalings are given by 1 −τ = N −α and T = N γT, with T > 0 fixed. The Thouless time TTh and the generalised Heisenberg time TH are indicated on the figure. (A) Dip–ramp– plateau structure of the DSFF across different time scales. (B) Universality crossover of the DSFF, showing GUE-type behaviour for γ ≤α and GinUE-type behaviour for α < γ < 1+α

Figure 2. DSFF in the regime of strong non-Hermiticity (α = 0) with τ = 0.3 and θ = π/6. Here, all axes are on a logarithmic scale. Top: DSFF for N = 64 (green, dashed), together with the analytic limits for γ = 0 (red, solid) and γ = 1

Figure 3. The plots illustrate the DSFF in the mesoscopic non-Hermiticity regime with pa- rameters N = 214, θ = π/6, and γ = 0.6, in all 3 figures. Accordingly, in case (A) with α < γ, one observes the quadratic ramp κT2
Figure 4. Decomposition of the positive real axis into the four asymptotic regimes for the Laguerre polynomials.

Figure 5. Each plot compares the asymptotic behaviour of fN in the corresponding regime. In the regimes I, II, and III, the dotted, dot-dashed, and dashed curves represent the remainder obtained after subtracting the expansion up to the second term, for N = 8, 16, and 32 respec- tively, while the black solid curve shows the third-order term. In the regime II, the oscillatory component of the third-order term is further subtracted due to its explicit dependence on N. In the regime IV, where fN is of the form fN = N −1ge−4Nh, the dotted, dot-dashed, and dashed curves represent −(4N)−1 log(NfN/g), whereas the black solid curve represents h.
